title: Unauthenticated cross-tenant registry disclosure via GET /api/auth/check-tenants (re-opens SEC-002, leaks schemaName)
description: The custom route GET /api/auth/check-tenants returns the ENTIRE tenant registry (companyName, crNumber, nitaqatActivity, planTier, regulatoryContext AND internal Postgres schemaName) plus the 10 most recent users across ALL tenants (email, name, role, tenantId) with NO authentication, no role check and no tenant scoping. This is the exact data SEC-002 was remediated for on the tRPC procedure auth.tenantsList (now gated to PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS with schemaName removed) — but this parallel REST door bypasses that fix entirely and additionally re-exposes schemaName, the internal isolation key SEC-002 explicitly removed. The path is not in the middleware matcher, so no rate-limit or edge guard applies either.
expectedBehavior: Cross-tenant registry data must be restricted to a true platform operator (same PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS boundary used by auth.tenantsList) and must never expose schemaName. Ideally this diagnostic route should be deleted.
actualBehavior: Any anonymous HTTP client receives every tenant's company name, CR number, plan tier and internal schemaName plus recent user emails/roles/tenantIds.

evidence.snippet:
regulatoryContext: t.regulatoryContext,
        schemaName: t.schemaName,
        createdAt: t.createdAt,

reproduction: curl https://<host>/api/auth/check-tenants -> 200 JSON listing all tenants (incl. schemaName) and recent users across every tenant, no cookie/token supplied.
technicalRisk: Full cross-tenant registry disclosure including the internal schema-per-tenant isolation key; enables targeting of other tenants' schemas and user enumeration.
businessRisk: Saudi PDPL personal-data breach, competitor intelligence (every customer's CR number, plan tier), and exposure of the isolation primitive. Directly negates the SEC-002 remediation the 90/100 score relied on.
recommendation: Delete apps/web/app/api/auth/check-tenants/route.ts. If a diagnostic is required, gate it on the same PLATFORM_ADMIN_EMAILS operator boundary as auth.tenantsList and never return schemaName. Add the path to the middleware matcher regardless.
workaround: Block /api/auth/check-tenants at the edge/CDN until removed.

title: Unauthenticated proxy to Qiwa government API via /api/qiwa (re-opens SEC-009 through a REST twin)
exploitability: moderate (requires QIWA_CLIENT_ID/SECRET configured to reach the government API)
description: The custom route /api/qiwa (POST/PUT/GET) authenticates to the Qiwa government platform with the server's QIWA_CLIENT_ID/SECRET and forwards the caller's raw JSON body to POST/PUT/GET https://api.qiwa.sa/api/v2/employees. It performs NO session check, NO role check and NO tenant resolution. The tRPC twin qiwa.sync was correctly re-gated to requireRole(super_admin, hr_manager, payroll_admin) under SEC-009, but this REST endpoint is a wide-open parallel door to the same government actions (create / update / query employee records). It is also absent from the middleware matcher, so no rate-limit applies.
expectedBehavior: Any government-filing/integration action must require an authenticated session with integrations/payroll authority (requireRole super_admin/hr_manager/payroll_admin), mirroring qiwa.sync. Prefer removing the REST route and routing the UI through the guarded tRPC procedure.
actualBehavior: An anonymous request to /api/qiwa is proxied straight to the Qiwa government API using the tenant's credentials; if credentials are unset it returns 500 'Qiwa credentials not configured' (fail-open on authz, fail-closed only on missing creds).
reproduction: With QIWA creds configured: curl -X POST https://<host>/api/qiwa -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{...employee...}' (no cookie) -> forwarded to the Qiwa government platform.
technicalRisk: Anonymous invocation of external government create/update/query actions using server-held credentials; abuse/SSRF-style relay of the tenant's Qiwa identity.
businessRisk: Wrongful or unauthorized government labor-contract actions attributable to the tenant; regulatory exposure. Undermines the SEC-009 remediation.
recommendation: Delete apps/web/app/api/qiwa/route.ts (its capability is fully covered by qiwa.sync), or add auth()+requireRole gating at the top of each handler and register the path in the middleware matcher.
workaround: Block /api/qiwa at the edge and/or leave QIWA credentials unset until fixed.

title: employee.getById / employee.list expose salary + payslips to roles lacking payroll:view_company; getById is not department-scoped
description: employee.list and employee.getById are companyProcedure (any staff role). The employees row carries plaintext salaryBasic/salaryHousing/salaryTransport columns and no column projection is applied, so recruiter and department_manager — who hold people:view_company but NOT payroll:view_company — read every employee's base salary. Worse, employee.getById additionally loads the payslips relation (full pay history) and, unlike employee.list, applies NO department scoping. A department_manager can therefore pass any employeeId to getById and read that employee's complete record plus payslip financials, defeating the SEC-013 department-scope intent for reads. The prior audit's permission matrix marked employee.list/getById as 'Secure', missing this.
expectedBehavior: Salary columns and the payslips relation should be gated to PAYROLL_VIEW_ROLES / payroll:view_company (projected out for other roles), and employee.getById should apply the same getManagedDepartmentIds scoping as employee.list for department_manager.
actualBehavior: recruiter and department_manager receive salaryBasic/Housing/Transport for all employees via list; department_manager receives any employee's full record + payslips via getById with no scope check.

evidence.snippet:
salaryBasic:     numeric("salary_basic",     { precision: 12, scale: 2 }).notNull(),
  salaryHousing:   numeric("salary_housing",   { precision: 12, scale: 2 }).notNull().default("0"),
  salaryTransport: numeric("salary_transport", { precision: 12, scale: 2 }).notNull().default("0"),

reproduction: Login as recruiter -> call employee.list -> response rows include salaryBasic/salaryHousing/salaryTransport. Login as department_manager -> call employee.getById with an employeeId outside your department -> returns that employee's full record + payslips.
technicalRisk: Within-tenant salary and pay-history confidentiality breach; department-scope bypass on the getById read path.
businessRisk: Staff without compensation authority learn colleagues' salaries; contradicts the capability model and SEC-013 scoping the audit claims complete.
recommendation: Add a column projection to employee.list/getById that omits salary fields for roles without payroll:view_company, gate the payslips relation on PAYROLL_VIEW_ROLES, and apply getManagedDepartmentIds scoping inside getById for department_manager.

title: Incomplete SEC-006 fix: attendance company reads and leave company reads remain companyProcedure (recruiter over-exposure)
url: attendance.list, attendance.monthlyReport, attendance.exceptions, attendance.assignment.list, leave.request.list, leave.balance.list
description: The Phase 2 SEC-006 remediation applied requireCapability to recruitment, retention, settlement, document and ai reads, but did NOT touch the attendance and leave company-wide reads. attendance.list/monthlyReport/exceptions/assignment.list and leave.request.list/balance.list are still companyProcedure, which authorizes any non-employee/candidate staff role. The capability model grants attendance:view_company and leave:view_company to super_admin/hr_manager/hr_specialist/payroll_admin/department_manager but NOT recruiter. A recruiter therefore reads all-employee attendance (attendance.list embeds each employee row and last workLocation) and all-employee leave, outside their capability set. The 03-permission-matrix.md itself flagged these as 'Over-permissive (recruiter)' — they were never closed.
expectedBehavior: attendance.list/monthlyReport/exceptions/assignment.list should be requireCapability('attendance:view_company') and leave.request.list/balance.list requireCapability('leave:view_company'), consistent with the rest of Phase 2.
actualBehavior: recruiter (no attendance/leave company capability) can read every employee's attendance and leave records.
reproduction: Login as recruiter -> call attendance.list or leave.request.list -> returns company-wide records despite recruiter lacking attendance:view_company / leave:view_company.
technicalRisk: Recruiter reads attendance (incl. last work location) and leave for the whole workforce.
businessRisk: Within-tenant confidentiality violation of the stated capability matrix; the SEC-006 'systemic fix' is incomplete.
recommendation: Swap companyProcedure -> requireCapability('attendance:view_company') on the four attendance reads and requireCapability('leave:view_company') on leave.request.list/balance.list (mirror the getSubtree fix already applied).

title: expense.markPaid callable by any staff role (finance action on companyProcedure)
description: expense.markPaid transitions an approved expense to 'paid' (a finance close-out) but is guarded only by companyProcedure, so every non-employee/candidate staff role — including recruiter and department_manager, who hold no finance/payroll capability — can mark expenses paid. The in-code comment claims 'Only HR / super_admin can do this', which the guard does not enforce. SEC-004 fixed the read path but left this write over-permissive; the 03-permission-matrix.md flagged it as 'Over-permissive (any staff role)'.
expectedBehavior: markPaid should be requireRole(super_admin, hr_manager, payroll_admin) / requireCapability('payroll:view_company') to match the finance-close intent.
actualBehavior: recruiter and department_manager can move expenses to paid state.
reproduction: Login as recruiter -> call expense.markPaid on an approved expense id -> status becomes 'paid'.
technicalRisk: Unauthorized financial state transition by non-finance staff.
businessRisk: Weak segregation of duties on expense payout; contradicts the documented finance-only intent.
recommendation: Change expense.markPaid to requireRole(super_admin, hr_manager, payroll_admin) (the EXPENSE_COMPANY_ROLES already defined in this file) or requireCapability.

title: Alternate login route /api/auth/login bypasses account lockout and rate limiting (brute-force oracle)
description: POST /api/auth/login performs a bcrypt password comparison against users.passwordHash and returns ok+user on success, but it bypasses every protection the NextAuth authorize() callback provides: the durable per-account lockout, the MFA gate, and the login rate-limit. The middleware only rate-limits /api/auth/callback/credentials (5/min) and general /api/auth (loose), and /api/auth/login is NOT in the middleware matcher at all, so no edge throttle applies. The route does not set a session cookie, so it is a password-verification oracle rather than a session bypass — but it enables unthrottled offline-style online password guessing that the account-lockout control (praised in the prior audit) was meant to stop.
expectedBehavior: There should be a single authentication path (NextAuth credentials) that enforces lockout, MFA and rate-limiting; ad-hoc credential-check routes should not exist, or must apply the same lockout/rate-limit logic.
actualBehavior: Unlimited unauthenticated password guesses against any account with no lockout, MFA or throttle.
reproduction: Script POST /api/auth/login with a fixed email and many passwords -> no lockout or 429 is ever returned; failed_login_attempts/locked_until are never consulted.
technicalRisk: Online password brute force with the account-lockout control bypassed; credential-stuffing amplifier.
businessRisk: Account takeover risk; the auth-hardening the 90/100 score credited is bypassable through this route.
recommendation: Remove /api/auth/login if unused; otherwise route it through the same authorize() logic (lockout + MFA + rate-limit) and add it to the middleware matcher's login rate-limit branch.
workaround: Add /api/auth/login to the login rate-limit matcher and block at edge.

title: recruitment.application.create and recruitment.referral.create trust full client payload on bare protectedProcedure (residual SEC-010)
role: any staff role (payroll_admin, department_manager, hr_specialist included)
description: SEC-010's create-spoofing sub-item was only partially remediated. offer.accept/decline, recognition.create/update and surveyResponse.create/update were fixed, but recruitment.application.create and recruitment.referral.create remain bare protectedProcedure that insert the raw client payload (including referrerEmployeeId) with no session binding and no recruitment:manage gate. Any staff role that passes canAccessProcedure (recruiter, but also payroll_admin, department_manager, hr_specialist) can create applications/referrals with a spoofed referrer to credit an arbitrary employee. It is not reachable by employee/candidate (not in their allowlists), so impact is staff-internal.
expectedBehavior: Bind referrer/owner ids to the session where applicable and gate creation to requireRole(super_admin, hr_manager, recruiter) (recruitment:manage), consistent with the other recruitment mutations.
actualBehavior: Staff roles without recruitment:manage can create applications/referrals with client-controlled owner fields.
reproduction: Login as payroll_admin -> call recruitment.referral.create with an arbitrary referrerEmployeeId -> row created crediting that employee.
technicalRisk: Data-integrity/spoofing on referral attribution and applications by roles lacking recruitment:manage.
businessRisk: Referral-reward fraud potential; minor within-tenant integrity issue.
recommendation: Change both to requireRole(super_admin, hr_manager, recruiter) and bind referrerEmployeeId to the session for self-referrals.

title: ai.assistant.list / getById remain companyProcedure (assistant config readable by all staff roles)
description: Phase 2 moved ai.assistant.create/update/delete to requireCapability('settings:manage') but left the corresponding reads (ai.assistant.list, ai.assistant.getById) on companyProcedure, so any staff role can read the AI assistant configuration (prompts, model settings). Low sensitivity, but inconsistent with the settings:manage gating applied to the writes.
expectedBehavior: Reads of settings-managed configuration should be requireCapability('settings:manage') (or at least align with who may manage them).
actualBehavior: Any staff role reads AI assistant configuration.
reproduction: Login as recruiter -> call ai.assistant.list -> returns assistant configs.
technicalRisk: Low; configuration disclosure to over-broad staff set.
recommendation: Gate ai.assistant.list/getById on requireCapability('settings:manage') to match the write path.

title: Debug endpoint /api/auth/debug leaks env/config metadata (production-gated)
description: GET /api/auth/debug returns AUTH_SECRET length, AUTH_URL/NEXTAUTH_URL values, DB host/port, NODE_ENV and DEMO_MODE. It is correctly disabled in production (returns 404 unless ENABLE_DEBUG_ENDPOINT === 'true'), so risk is limited to non-production environments. Recorded for completeness; if a non-prod environment is internet-reachable this leaks the auth-secret length and DB host.
expectedBehavior: Diagnostic endpoints should not be shipped as public HTTP routes; if kept, restrict by network/auth even outside production.
actualBehavior: In non-production, anonymous callers receive env/config metadata.
reproduction: In a non-prod deployment: curl /api/auth/debug -> 200 JSON with authSecretLength, dbHost, etc.
technicalRisk: Minor metadata disclosure feeding secret/side-channel guessing in non-prod.
recommendation: Delete the route or require an operator token even in non-production; never expose AUTH_SECRET length or DB host.
workaround: Ensure NODE_ENV=production and ENABLE_DEBUG_ENDPOINT unset in all internet-reachable deployments.
